'''Dale Laughton''' (born 10 October 1970) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], and at club level for [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]], as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Laughton played {{rlp|pr}} in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998. That year he was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]].<ref>{{cite news 
|last = Hadfield
|first = Dave
|title = League proposes show in S Africa
|work = [[The Independent]]
|place =UK
|date = 23 September 1998
|url = https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-league-proposes-show-in-s-africa-1200080.html
|accessdate = 24 April 2010}}</ref> Laughton was selected to travel with the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain team]] down under for the [[1999 Rugby League Tri-Nations|1999 Tri-Nations]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He later played for the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland national team]] international and played at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].

Laughton retired from playing in January 2003,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/warrington-to-part-with-busby-and-laughton-125718.html |title=Warrington to part with Busby and Laughton |newspaper=The Independent |date=24 January 2003 |access-date=8 August 2024}}</ref> and re-joined Sheffield Eagles in a coaching role.<ref>{{cite web |title=Laughton back with Eagles |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/2707195.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=18 August 2023 |date=29 January 2003}}</ref>
